title: United Kingdom Child Benefit Claim (Form CH2)
description: Claim Child Benefit from HM Revenue and Customs (HMRC) using the paper CH2 claim form, for a parent/guardian claiming for the first time or adding another child to an existing claim who cannot or does not want to use the online/HMRC app channel. Models the base two-column CH2 form: About you, where you have lived and your immigration status, your marital/civil-partnership status, about your partner, up to 2 children claimed for (a claim for 3 or more children requires the form's separate 'Additional children' continuation page), the High Income Child Benefit Charge opt-in/opt-out question for higher-income households, how you want to be paid, bank details, and the declaration. Does not model: the online/HMRC-app claim channel (which does not publish a field-by-field form outside an authenticated session), the 'Additional children' continuation page for a 3rd or subsequent child, reporting a change of circumstances to an existing claim, or cancelling/objecting to a decision. Does not submit the claim; the live gov.uk 'Claim Child Benefit' online service and the postal CH2 form are always authoritative.

source.documentRef: CH2 (HMRC 04/26) — Child Benefit claim form, with companion CH2 Notes ('Getting your claim right') and CH3 address label

verification.notes: Directly downloadable from assets.publishing.service.gov.uk (no bot-block encountered), current HMRC 04/26 edition (April 2026 — the most recent edition, not a retired/mirrored one). Both the claim form PDF and its companion CH2 Notes PDF were fetched via curl (HTTP 200) and text-extracted with a scratch npm install of pdfjs-dist (legacy build); both are plain, non-encrypted PDFs whose standard text-layer extraction recovered the full form. Status remains 'draft' pending a live confirmation that the online/HMRC-app claim channel collects the same field set (that channel requires a Government Gateway sign-in and was not walked through this cycle). See VERIFICATION.md.

fields.name: isHigherIncomeHousehold
fields.label: Does either you or your partner have an individual Adjusted Net Income (ANI) of more than £60,000 a year?
fields.description: Gates Q62; ANI is an individual's total taxable income including any taxable benefits, before Personal Allowances and minus certain tax reliefs.
fields.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, page 7 'Only answer question 62 if either you or your partner have an individual Adjusted Net Income (ANI) of more than £60,000 a year'

fields.name: wantsToBePaidChildBenefit
fields.label: Do you want to be paid Child Benefit?
fields.description: If No: you do not want to be paid Child Benefit, but want to protect your State Pension. If Yes: you want to be paid and understand you or your partner may have to pay an Income Tax charge (the High Income Child Benefit Charge).
fields.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, Q62 'Do you want to be paid Child Benefit?'

fields.name: alreadyGetsChildBenefit
fields.label: Do you already get Child Benefit?
fields.description: If Yes, HMRC uses the bank/building society account already used to pay your Child Benefit and questions 63-71 are skipped.
fields.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, Q65 'Do you already get Child Benefit?'

fields.name: wantsWeeklyPayment
fields.label: Do you want to be paid Child Benefit every week?
fields.description: Child Benefit is normally paid every 4 weeks into a bank or building society account.
fields.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, Q63 'Do you want to be paid Child Benefit every week?'

fields.name: weeklyPaymentEligibilitySingleParent
fields.label: Weekly payment eligibility — I am a single parent
fields.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, Q64 'I am a single parent'

fields.name: weeklyPaymentEligibilityQualifyingBenefit
fields.label: Weekly payment eligibility — I or my partner receive a qualifying benefit
fields.description: Qualifying benefits: Income Support, Income-based Jobseeker's Allowance, Pension Credit, Income-related Employment and Support Allowance, or Universal Credit.
fields.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, Q64 'I or my partner receive one or more of the following: Income Support / Income-based Jobseeker's Allowance / Pension Credit / Income-related Employment and Support Allowance / Universal Credit'

fields.name: accountHolderType
fields.label: Whose name is the payment account in?
fields.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, Q66 'The account is in my name / The account is in someone else's name / The account is in joint names'

fields.name: accountHolderOtherName1
fields.label: Account holder name 1 (if not your own account)

fields.name: accountHolderOtherName2
fields.label: Account holder name 2 (joint account)

fields.name: bankOrBuildingSocietyName
fields.label: Name of the bank or building society
fields.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, Q67 'Name of the bank or building society'

fields.name: bankSortCode
fields.label: Branch sort code
fields.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, Q68 'The branch sort code'

fields.name: bankAccountNumber
fields.label: Account number
fields.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, Q69 'The account number'

fields.name: buildingSocietyRollNumber
fields.label: Building society roll or reference number, if you have it
fields.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, Q70 'If the account is with a building society, tell us the roll or reference number if you have it'

fields.name: noAccountToPayInto
fields.label: You do not have an account that HMRC can pay into
fields.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, Q71 "If you do not have an account that we can pay into, put an 'X' in this box"

fields.name: sendingRequiredDocuments
fields.label: Sending the birth certificates or other required documents with this form (not photocopies)
fields.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, Declaration "I am sending the birth certificates or other documents that you need with this form. I am not sending photocopies."

fields.name: declaration
fields.label: Declaration — information given is correct and complete
fields.description: Declares the information given is correct and complete, understands that giving incorrect/incomplete information may result in action being taken, and undertakes to report changes of circumstances, understanding an overpayment may need to be repaid if not reported.
fields.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, Declaration "I declare that the information I've given on this form is correct and complete..."

fields.name: declarationDate
fields.label: Declaration date
fields.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, Declaration 'Date'

documents.label: Child 1 — birth certificate
documents.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, page 4 'Documents we need — If the child's birth was registered in Northern Ireland or outside the UK, send us a birth certificate for each child in this claim.'

documents.label: Child 1 — passport or travel document used to enter the UK
documents.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, page 4 'If any child in this claim was born outside the UK we also need to see their passports or their travel documents used to enter the UK.'

documents.label: Child 1 — adoption certificate
documents.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, page 4 'If your child was adopted abroad, you should send us the adoption certificate.'

documents.label: Child 2 — passport or travel document used to enter the UK
documents.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, page 4 'passports or their travel documents used to enter the UK'

documents.label: Child 2 — adoption certificate
documents.sourceRef: CH2 04/26, page 4 'If your child was adopted abroad, you should send us the adoption certificate.'
